verses.text: God tells the grieving Samuel, 'How long wilt thou mourn for Saul?' and sends him to anoint a new king among Jesse's sons. There is a time to stop weeping over what God has set aside and to move on to what He is doing next. God's work does not stall at our sorrow.

God tells the grieving Samuel, 'How long wilt thou mourn for Saul?' and sends him to anoint a new king among Jesse's sons. There is a time to stop weeping over what God has set aside and to move on to what He is doing next. God's work does not stall at our sorrow.

God tells the grieving Samuel, 'How long wilt thou mourn for Saul?' and sends him to anoint a new king among Jesse's sons. There is a time to stop weeping over what God has set aside and to move on to what He is doing next. God's work does not stall at our sorrow.

Samuel comes to Bethlehem and the elders tremble, unsure if he brings judgment. He calls them instead to a sacrifice: 'sanctify yourselves.' Before God reveals His choice, hearts are made ready. The great things of God are shown to the consecrated, not the careless.

Samuel comes to Bethlehem and the elders tremble, unsure if he brings judgment. He calls them instead to a sacrifice: 'sanctify yourselves.' Before God reveals His choice, hearts are made ready. The great things of God are shown to the consecrated, not the careless.

Seeing tall Eliab, Samuel thinks, 'surely this is he.' But God says, 'man looketh on the outward appearance, but the LORD looketh on the heart.' God's choices overturn ours. He is never impressed by what impresses us; He weighs what no human eye can see.

Seeing tall Eliab, Samuel thinks, 'surely this is he.' But God says, 'man looketh on the outward appearance, but the LORD looketh on the heart.' God's choices overturn ours. He is never impressed by what impresses us; He weighs what no human eye can see.

One by one seven sons of Jesse pass before Samuel, and the LORD has chosen none of them. God will not be hurried into the obvious. When every visible candidate is set aside, it is because His eye is on someone the room has not even thought to call.

One by one seven sons of Jesse pass before Samuel, and the LORD has chosen none of them. God will not be hurried into the obvious. When every visible candidate is set aside, it is because His eye is on someone the room has not even thought to call.

One by one seven sons of Jesse pass before Samuel, and the LORD has chosen none of them. God will not be hurried into the obvious. When every visible candidate is set aside, it is because His eye is on someone the room has not even thought to call.

The youngest, David, is fetched from the sheep, and the LORD says, 'Arise, anoint him.' As the oil flows, 'the Spirit of the LORD came upon David from that day forward.' God chooses the overlooked shepherd boy and equips the one He calls. His grace finds us where we are.

The youngest, David, is fetched from the sheep, and the LORD says, 'Arise, anoint him.' As the oil flows, 'the Spirit of the LORD came upon David from that day forward.' God chooses the overlooked shepherd boy and equips the one He calls. His grace finds us where we are.

The youngest, David, is fetched from the sheep, and the LORD says, 'Arise, anoint him.' As the oil flows, 'the Spirit of the LORD came upon David from that day forward.' God chooses the overlooked shepherd boy and equips the one He calls. His grace finds us where we are.

Meanwhile the Spirit departs from Saul, and a tormenting spirit fills the void. A soul that pushes God out does not stay empty; something darker moves in. Saul's servants seek a harp to soothe him — treating the symptom while the true sickness goes unaddressed.

Meanwhile the Spirit departs from Saul, and a tormenting spirit fills the void. A soul that pushes God out does not stay empty; something darker moves in. Saul's servants seek a harp to soothe him — treating the symptom while the true sickness goes unaddressed.

Saul sends for David, the skilful harpist of Bethlehem, never guessing he is calling his own appointed successor into his house. God weaves His purposes through ordinary needs. The very king David will one day replace now summons him to his side.

Saul sends for David, the skilful harpist of Bethlehem, never guessing he is calling his own appointed successor into his house. God weaves His purposes through ordinary needs. The very king David will one day replace now summons him to his side.

Saul sends for David, the skilful harpist of Bethlehem, never guessing he is calling his own appointed successor into his house. God weaves His purposes through ordinary needs. The very king David will one day replace now summons him to his side.

David comes, and his playing brings Saul relief, so that Saul loves him and makes him his armourbearer. The anointed king serves the rejected one humbly, waiting on God's timing. David does not grasp the crown; he plays his harp and bides his time under the hand of God.
